Package org.solenopsis.soap.service

Enum Class ServiceWsdlEnum

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<ServiceWsdlEnum>
org.solenopsis.soap.service.ServiceWsdlEnum
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<ServiceWsdlEnum>, Constable
public enum ServiceWsdlEnum extends Enum<ServiceWsdlEnum>
Enum holding URLs to Salesforce WSDL (Web Services Description Language) files.

WSDL files define the structure and operations available in SOAP web services. This enum loads the WSDL files from the classpath as resources, making them available for SOAP client generation and runtime service creation.

The WSDL files are located in src/main/resources/wsdl/ and are packaged with the library JAR.

Author:
sfloess

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    APEX
    Apex API WSDL for executing anonymous Apex code.
    ENTERPRISE
    Enterprise API WSDL for strongly-typed data operations.
    METADATA
    Metadata API WSDL for deploying and retrieving metadata.
    PARTNER
    Partner API WSDL for dynamically-typed data operations.
    TOOLING
    Tooling API WSDL for developer tools and schema introspection.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static ServiceWsdlEnum[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static ServiceWsdlEnum valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • getUrl

      public URL getUrl()
      Returns the URL pointing to the WSDL file.

      This URL can be used to create SOAP services at runtime or to access the WSDL definition programmatically.

      Returns:
      the URL to the WSDL file (never null)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the WSDL resource is not found on the classpath
